name: Amber Cascade
placementNotes: Works best centered in a formal living room or foyer. The cascading amber glass drops catch and scatter warm light beautifully; pair with gold-toned accents and cream or ivory furnishings.
scaleReference: Approximately 50–60cm diameter, medium room scale

name: Antler Crown
placementNotes: Designed for double-height foyers or grand stairwells where its dramatic vertical height can be fully appreciated. Avoid low ceilings — needs at least 12ft clearance.
scaleReference: Very tall sculptural piece, approximately 120–150cm height, grand scale

name: Aurum Frond
placementNotes: Designed for double-height foyers and open stairwells; the oval canopy with cascading twisted glass fronds creates a dramatic statement. Lit from built-in ceiling spots as well as the suspended elements.
scaleReference: Large oval canopy approximately 100cm wide, drops cascade 80–120cm below

name: Barrel Rustic
placementNotes: Perfect over a rustic or farmhouse dining table or on a covered terrace. The whitewashed barrel cage gives a relaxed, outdoor-living feel; works well in pairs for long tables.
scaleReference: Approximately 45cm diameter, medium chandelier scale

name: Bead Cascade
placementNotes: Striking contrast of matte black scrollwork arms and cascading crystal fringe makes this a glamorous focal point. Ideal over a seating area or in a formal dining room with dark or jewel-toned walls.
scaleReference: Approximately 70cm wide, large chandelier with generous crystal drop

name: Brass Bloom Cluster
placementNotes: Five globe pendants suspended from an ornate brass medallion canopy — stunning in a foyer or double-height entry. The decorative ceiling plate is as much a feature as the pendants themselves.
scaleReference: Canopy approximately 60cm wide, globes approximately 25–30cm diameter each

name: Brass Blossom Cluster
placementNotes: Classic multi-arm chandelier with fabric shades — ideal over a formal dining table or in a traditional drawing room. Pairs beautifully with warm wood furniture and earthy or cream tones.
scaleReference: Approximately 70cm spread, 6-arm medium chandelier

name: Brass Bough
placementNotes: An oversized sculptural statement in matte black iron with intertwining curved arms and upward-facing cap lights — ideal for a grand dining room or open-plan space. Needs significant ceiling height and open volume.
scaleReference: Very large, approximately 150–180cm wide linear span, architectural scale

name: Brass Dome Cluster
placementNotes: Three ornate brass bodies each with four glass-domed shades, suspended in a linear row — perfect over a long dining table or kitchen island. The glass shades provide excellent diffused downward light.
scaleReference: Each unit approximately 40cm wide; three-unit linear row spans approximately 150cm

name: Brass Dome Pendant
placementNotes: A refined single pendant with a flattened glass dome housing an antique brass multi-light interior — suits foyers, hallways, and powder rooms. Use in a pair for passages or flanking a console.
scaleReference: Approximately 40–45cm diameter dome, medium pendant scale

name: Brass Facet Long
placementNotes: The tall faceted geometric form with antique mirror-glass panels makes a dramatic statement in a foyer or stairwell nook. Hang at a height where the full elongated silhouette is visible.
scaleReference: Approximately 35cm wide, 60–70cm tall, elongated pendant

name: Brass Facet Short
placementNotes: The shorter, rounder sibling of the Brass Facet Long — suits foyers and hallways with standard ceiling heights. The antique mirror glass panels create jewel-like reflections when lit.
scaleReference: Approximately 40–45cm diameter globe, compact statement pendant

name: Brass Geometric Cluster
placementNotes: Stacked sculptural cluster of geometric brass dome shades with fringe detailing at multiple heights — a bold statement for double-height foyers or grand living spaces. Needs significant vertical clearance.
scaleReference: Cluster spans approximately 60–70cm wide, total drop approximately 150cm

name: Brass Hexagon Lantern
placementNotes: A classic hexagonal brass lantern with clear glass panels and candle-style lights inside — timeless in a foyer or hallway. Pair with a matching wall lantern on either side of an entryway for a grand colonial look.
scaleReference: Approximately 30cm wide, 50cm tall lantern body, medium scale

name: Brass Lantern Cascade
placementNotes: Best suited for double-height foyers or grand stairwells where the cascading cluster of lanterns at varying heights creates dramatic visual impact. Pair with a carved console table and floor mirror below.
scaleReference: Cluster spans approximately 3–4 ft wide; individual lanterns are 14–18 inches tall

name: Brass Lantern Tiered
placementNotes: Hang over a rectangular dining table or kitchen island; the two-tier ribbed glass construction diffuses warm light evenly across the surface below. Works beautifully in rooms with warm wood tones.
scaleReference: Approximately 48 inches long × 14 inches wide × 12 inches tall

name: Brass Lattice
placementNotes: Ideal over a long dining table or in a formal living area; the double-tiered ribbed glass panels create warm, glowing diffused light. Complements rooms with panelled walls and layered brass accents.
scaleReference: Approximately 54–60 inches long × 16 inches wide × 14 inches tall

name: Brass Lattice Linear
placementNotes: Perfect over a long dining or conference table; the lattice-patterned brass canopy and ribbed-glass double-box body emit warm, diffused downlight. Suits transitional and Art Deco dining rooms.
scaleReference: Approximately 48–54 inches long × 14 inches wide × 12 inches tall

name: Brass Linear Cluster
placementNotes: Six glass-cylinder pendants drop from a brass lattice linear canopy — perfect over a dining table or bar counter. The exposed Edison-style bulbs add warmth and an industrial-heritage crossover aesthetic.
scaleReference: Canopy approximately 40 inches long; pendants hang 18–24 inches below

name: Crystal Crown
placementNotes: Best placed as a centrepiece in a formal drawing room or foyer; the shell-motif crown and cascading crystal drops create brilliant sparkle. Pair with antique gold wall sconces for a cohesive classical look.
scaleReference: Approximately 18 inches diameter × 20 inches tall

name: Shield
placementNotes: Hang in a foyer corner or at the top of a stairwell; the urn-shaped body with sunburst medallions, lattice cutwork, and tassel finial makes a strong sculptural statement without overwhelming the space.
scaleReference: Approximately 10 inches wide × 22 inches tall (excluding chain)

name: Sunburst
placementNotes: A wide dome chandelier with sunburst and floral medallion cutwork; works beautifully as a centrepiece in formal drawing rooms or over a round dining table. The open metalwork allows warm light to play across walls and ceilings.
scaleReference: Approximately 22–24 inches diameter × 16 inches tall

name: Brass Petals
placementNotes: A bold sculptural centrepiece; the oversized brushed-brass petal form radiates outward from a central globe, making it ideal as the focal point of a living room or master bedroom. Keeps surrounding decor minimal.
scaleReference: Approximately 30–36 inches diameter

name: Brass Ribbed Cylinder
placementNotes: A refined cylinder pendant with vertical ribbed glass panels held within polished brass hoops; suits hallways, powder rooms, or over a small dining setting. Works well in pairs or threes for corridor installations.
scaleReference: Approximately 12 inches diameter × 14 inches tall

name: Cherub Botanical
placementNotes: A grand antique chandelier with cherub figures, botanical scrollwork arms, and frosted floral glass shades; suited for heritage-style bungalows, formal dining rooms, or grand stairwells. Needs generous ceiling height and space.
scaleReference: Approximately 28–32 inches diameter × 30 inches tall

name: Crystal Bell Pendant
placementNotes: An elegant elongated crystal pendant with a scalloped bell shade and stacked crystal column body; works beautifully in pairs flanking a foyer or cascading in a stairwell. Use solo in a powder room for maximum drama.
scaleReference: Approximately 8 inches diameter shade × 36–48 inches total drop

name: Crystal Blossom
placementNotes: A spectacular branching chandelier with crystal blossoms in multicolor gemstone tones and cascading crystal drops beneath; ideal as a centrepiece in a vibrant formal room or over a dining table. Pair with rooms featuring bold accent colours.
scaleReference: Approximately 36–42 inches wide × 20 inches tall body

name: Crystal Bough
placementNotes: Sweeping chrome-silver scrolled branches adorned with crystal droplets and candle-style arms create a dramatic organic silhouette; best placed in a high-ceilinged formal room or double-height foyer where the branching form can be appreciated fully.
scaleReference: Approximately 36–40 inches diameter × 28 inches tall

name: Crystal Cascade Chandelier
placementNotes: Best suited for a formal living room or dining room with high ceilings. The cascading crystal strands and fabric shades create a dramatic focal point; pair with an antique sideboard or console beneath.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90 cm diameter, 60–70 cm height; suits rooms 14 ft+ wide

name: Crystal Cascade Cluster
placementNotes: A cluster of three cylindrical crystal-rod pendants installed at staggered heights makes a bold statement over a large dining table or in a double-height foyer. The antique brass frames complement dark wood ceilings.
scaleReference: Each drum approximately 50–60 cm diameter; cluster spans roughly 120 cm across

name: Crystal Cascade Trio
placementNotes: Three matching crystal-rod drum pendants hung at the same height work beautifully over a long dining table or island. The geometric lattice connectors add an Art Deco touch.
scaleReference: Each drum approximately 45–50 cm diameter; trio spans roughly 150 cm linearly

name: Crystal Cylinder
placementNotes: The large fabric drum shade with a crystal fringe tier beneath suits bedrooms and intimate dining areas. Works well in pairs flanking a bed or over a small round dining table.
scaleReference: Approximately 50 cm diameter drum shade, 40–50 cm total drop

name: Crystal Dome
placementNotes: This beaded crystal dome pendant is ideal as a jewel-like accent in a foyer, powder room, or bedroom corner. The compact dome shape and gilt brass frame suit spaces where a statement is needed without overwhelming scale.
scaleReference: Approximately 35–40 cm diameter, 35 cm height

name: Crystal Dome Pendant
placementNotes: A statement heritage lantern best placed in a grand foyer or stairwell with tall ceilings. The urn shape with ornate brass swag detailing and diamond-cut crystal body commands attention as a solo installation.
scaleReference: Approximately 35 cm diameter, 70–80 cm height including fittings

name: Solo
placementNotes: This elegant solo pendant with scrollwork crown and cascading crystal fringe tiers works beautifully in a foyer corner or over a console table. Can also be used singly in a bedroom or hallway nook.
scaleReference: Approximately 35 cm diameter, 55–65 cm height

name: Trio
placementNotes: Three fringe pendants clustered together from a shared canopy create an opulent installation for a double-height foyer or stairwell. The staggered heights add drama; pair with a large statement mirror below.
scaleReference: Cluster spans approximately 90–100 cm; individual pendants 35 cm diameter

name: Amber Prism
placementNotes: The amber glass shades and silver patina scrollwork arms give this chandelier a warm, eclectic glow. Best in a bar area, eclectic living room, or dining space where warm amber light enhances the atmosphere.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90 cm diameter, 60 cm height

name: Antique Scroll
placementNotes: This heavily scrolled, distressed-white chandelier with crystal drops suits a classically styled formal living room or foyer. Works especially well against neutral or muted wall tones to let the ornate silhouette stand out.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100 cm diameter, 60–70 cm height

name: Bloom
placementNotes: A floral-leaf candelabra chandelier with a romantic, distressed silver finish. Ideal for a master bedroom or a feminine formal living room. The open frame keeps it light while the crystal drops add sparkle.
scaleReference: Approximately 55–65 cm diameter, 55 cm height

name: Floral Scroll
placementNotes: A grand floral scroll chandelier in distressed white and silver with abundant crystal drops. Commands attention in a double-height foyer or large formal living room. Pair with white or cream interiors for maximum impact.
scaleReference: Approximately 100–120 cm diameter, 80–90 cm height

name: Crystal Lantern
placementNotes: A tall cylindrical lantern with intricate cut-crystal panels and a decorative crystal leaf crown at the top. Best used as a grand solo statement in a double-height foyer or at a stairwell. The elaborate cut patterns cast beautiful refractions.
scaleReference: Approximately 35–40 cm diameter, 90–100 cm height

name: Crystal Linear
placementNotes: A rectangular crystal-rod linear chandelier designed to hang directly over a long dining table. The uniform crystal rods and antique brass frame create a refined, contemporary look that suits both modern and transitional interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 120–140 cm length, 25–30 cm width, 20–25 cm height

name: Crystal Shell Cascade
placementNotes: Best suited for a foyer or formal living room ceiling. The cascading shell-shaped crystal globes create a dramatic downward focal point; pair with a statement console table below.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70cm wide, medium-hanging depth

name: Crystal Teardrop
placementNotes: Sold as individual pendants; works best grouped in threes at varying heights in a double-height foyer or stairwell. The all-crystal teardrop body casts prismatic light across walls.
scaleReference: Each pendant approximately 50–65cm tall, 35cm wide

name: Deco Lantern
placementNotes: Ideal for a dramatic foyer or hallway with high ceilings. The tiered geometric glass panels diffuse light warmly; hang solo as a bold architectural statement.
scaleReference: Approximately 40cm wide, 80–90cm tall body

name: Baroque
placementNotes: The open baroque scroll frame works beautifully in a French Provincial or Colonial dining room. Pair with a long dining table and matching wall sconces for a complete look.
scaleReference: Approximately 65–70cm tall, 55cm wide

name: Shield
placementNotes: The shield-shaped open frame with scroll scrollwork suits a rustic or farmhouse foyer. Works well hung in a corner alcove or over a small entryway console.
scaleReference: Approximately 50cm wide, 65cm tall

name: Dome Crystal
placementNotes: A grand two-tiered statement piece for formal dining rooms or double-height foyers. The crystal ball centrepiece and drop pendants require ample ceiling height; place centrally over a large dining table.
scaleReference: Approximately 100–120cm wide, multi-tiered drop

name: Dome Scroll
placementNotes: The broad dome base with radiating scroll arms suits a formal dining room centred over a large table. The upward-facing candelabra arms cast warm ambient light across the ceiling.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100cm wide, moderate drop

name: Cascade
placementNotes: The swag bead garlands draped across brass arms give this chandelier a relaxed farmhouse elegance. Centre over a long dining table or hang in a high-ceiling living room as a statement focal point.
scaleReference: Approximately 90cm wide, medium cascading drop

name: Crown
placementNotes: A more compact farmhouse bead chandelier with matte black iron arms and whitewashed wood column. Works well over a round dining table or in a bedroom with boho-rustic decor.
scaleReference: Approximately 65–70cm wide, standard drop

name: Fern Nocturne
placementNotes: The sprawling botanical branch arms with frosted globe shades make this ideal over a long dining table or as a ceiling sculpture in a living room. The wide spread requires ample horizontal clearance.
scaleReference: Approximately 120–140cm wide, low-profile drop

name: Fluted Scroll
placementNotes: The fluted glass shades on scrolled antique brass arms create a classic, warm glow. Works well over a dining table or in a formal living room paired with matching wall sconces.
scaleReference: Approximately 75–80cm wide, standard drop

name: Foliate Cascade
placementNotes: The palm-frond brass arms with cascading crystal drop fringe create a tropical-luxe statement. Best in a foyer or formal living room; the wide spread needs open ceiling space.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90cm wide, low-to-medium drop

name: Foliate Crown
placementNotes: The foliate brass crown with cut-crystal globe shades suits a formal living room or traditional dining space. Pairs beautifully with antique-finish wall sconces and rich textile furnishings.
scaleReference: Approximately 65–75cm wide, medium drop

name: Glass Bell Pendant
placementNotes: Best used in clusters of two or three over a kitchen island, bar counter, or in a passageway. The clear glass bell with matte black hardware suits contemporary and transitional interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 30cm wide, 45–55cm tall

name: Golden Curve Chandelier
placementNotes: Best suited above a central seating area or foyer. The open circular ring frame and curved arms read well in rooms with clean white or off-white ceilings.
scaleReference: Approximately 70–80cm diameter; mid-sized chandelier suitable for rooms 12–16ft wide

name: Crown
placementNotes: Ideal for double-height foyers, grand stairwells, or arched alcoves. The elaborate crown top and dark patina finish command presence in tall, heritage-style spaces.
scaleReference: Approximately 45–55cm diameter, 70–90cm tall body; grand statement piece requiring high ceilings

name: Cylinder
placementNotes: A monumental piece for grand double-height foyers or hotel-lobby-scale spaces. The floor-length form and ornate brass crown demand at least 14ft ceilings; pair with arched architecture.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70cm diameter, 150–200cm tall; suitable only for very large volumes

name: Glass
placementNotes: Versatile mid-sized brass lantern that works in foyers, hallways, and intimate dining rooms. The curved glass panels diffuse light warmly and the antique brass finish ages beautifully.
scaleReference: Approximately 35–40cm diameter, 55–65cm tall; suitable for standard ceiling heights

name: Pavilion
placementNotes: Designed for grand foyers and stairwell voids. The enclosed pavilion structure with a full interior candelabra creates a dramatic glowing box effect — powerful in double-height Heritage or Colonial interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 55–65cm diameter, 100–130cm tall body; requires substantial ceiling clearance

name: Acanthus Crown
placementNotes: The acanthus-leaf detailing and cream fabric shades make this a refined centerpiece for formal living or dining rooms. Works best in rooms with classical moldings and warm-toned furnishings.
scaleReference: Approximately 75–85cm diameter; large-format chandelier for rooms 14–18ft wide

name: Baroque Foliage
placementNotes: The layered baroque scrollwork arms and cascading crystal drops make this a show-stopping piece for formal dining rooms or grand living areas. The scalloped cream shades soften the ornate frame beautifully.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100cm diameter; statement piece requiring ample ceiling height and room volume

name: Curved Linen
placementNotes: A versatile transitional chandelier with curved arms and scalloped linen shades. Works well centered over a dining table or in a living room with warm wood and neutral tones.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70cm diameter; mid-sized piece for rooms 12–16ft wide

name: Drum Luminaire
placementNotes: A compact, understated chandelier ideal for bedrooms, smaller dining rooms, or studies. The slim antique brass arms and cream shades provide warm, diffused light without overwhelming the space.
scaleReference: Approximately 50–60cm diameter; suitable for smaller rooms or lower ceilings

name: Frosted Scroll
placementNotes: The combination of scrolled brass arms, frosted fabric shades, and crystal drops creates a classically glamorous look. Ideal for formal dining rooms or living rooms with traditional decor.
scaleReference: Approximately 70–80cm diameter; substantial mid-sized piece for formal spaces

name: Gilt Ornament
placementNotes: A monumental three-tier chandelier for grand double-height foyers and palatial living spaces. The sheer scale and layered scrollwork arms with crystal drops and white mini shades make it the ultimate statement piece.
scaleReference: Approximately 120–140cm diameter, 100–120cm tall; requires double-height ceilings of 14ft or more

name: Gilt Plume
placementNotes: The golden pleated fabric shades and cascading crystal chains give this chandelier a rich, opulent feel. Best placed in formal dining rooms or traditional living spaces with warm wood tones and textured walls.
scaleReference: Approximately 75–85cm diameter; substantial piece suited to rooms 14–18ft wide

name: Ivory Descent
placementNotes: A grand two-tier chandelier with ivory scalloped shades and generous crystal drops. Ideal for large formal living rooms or foyers with high ceilings. The ivory shades keep it from feeling too dark despite the antique brass frame.
scaleReference: Approximately 100–110cm diameter; grand-scale piece requiring 12ft+ ceilings and large rooms

name: Prism Drift
placementNotes: A refined Heritage Shade chandelier with cream fabric shades, crystal prism drops, and a distinctive globe finial at the base. Works beautifully in formal dining rooms or traditional living rooms with antique brass accents.
scaleReference: Approximately 75–85cm diameter; suited to rooms 14–16ft wide with 9–11ft ceilings

name: Scrolled Orb
placementNotes: Best in a formal living room or grand foyer with high ceilings. Pair with antique brass wall sconces and a console table below for a layered look.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90cm diameter; suits rooms 14ft+ wide

name: Scrolled Prism
placementNotes: Ideal as a statement piece in a master bedroom or intimate dining room. The dark frame and glowing shades create a moody, romantic atmosphere.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70cm diameter; compact enough for bedrooms

name: Scrollwork Radiance
placementNotes: Suited for grand living rooms or formal dining spaces. The ornate scrollwork and dangling crystal drops make it a focal point; keep surrounding décor restrained.
scaleReference: Approximately 75–85cm diameter; substantial presence in medium-to-large rooms

name: Hexagon Cascade Rectangular
placementNotes: Designed to float above a long dining table or in a double-height reception hall. The stepped rectangular tiers create a dramatic Art Deco silhouette when lit.
scaleReference: Approximately 120–150cm length; suits dining tables 8–10 seats long

name: Hexagon Cascade Square
placementNotes: Best centered over a square dining table or in a grand living room. The stepped square tiers give a pagoda-like drama; keep walls and furniture minimal.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–110cm square footprint; works well in 12ft+ ceiling rooms

name: Hexagon Lantern
placementNotes: Perfect in an entry foyer, stairwell, or covered terrace. The hexagonal glass panels allow the candle-style bulbs to sparkle from all angles.
scaleReference: Approximately 25cm wide × 50cm tall; slender profile fits narrow passages

name: Cage
placementNotes: Works as a centrepiece above a round dining table or in a bar area. The open cage silhouette keeps the look airy; the brass fittings add warmth to the matte black frame.
scaleReference: Approximately 70–80cm diameter; bold presence without bulk

name: Ring
placementNotes: Ideal above a large round or oval dining table. The clean iron ring with brass candle cups creates an understated yet striking silhouette; suits loft-style or modern-rustic interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100cm diameter ring; works in rooms 14ft+ wide

name: Iron Scroll
placementNotes: Designed to command a grand foyer or double-height living space. The bold shield-shaped iron outline with internal brass scrollwork makes it a sculptural statement; avoid competing with busy wallpaper.
scaleReference: Approximately 80cm wide × 100cm tall; highly vertical, suits double-height ceilings

name: Jewel Cascade
placementNotes: A conversation-starter for an eclectic living room or vibrant foyer. Pair with bold, colourful accent pillows and a neutral sofa so the chandelier remains the undisputed hero.
scaleReference: Approximately 60cm diameter × 90cm drop; colour-saturated and attention-commanding

name: Jeweled Curve
placementNotes: Best suited to a grand formal living room or large dining space. The multi-arm brass frame with fabric shades and colourful hanging glass clusters adds a playful glamour; works well in cream or gold interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100cm diameter; fills large ceiling spans gracefully

name: Lantern Cluster
placementNotes: Dramatic when hung at varied heights in a double-height foyer or stairwell void. The cluster of tapered glass lanterns creates a signature entry statement; use at staggered drop lengths for maximum impact.
scaleReference: Cluster spans approximately 80–100cm wide; individual lanterns 30–50cm tall

name: Leaf Cascade
placementNotes: Conceived for grand double-height spaces — a formal living room, luxury hotel-style foyer, or stairwell void. The sweeping gilded arms with cascading crystal drops are best viewed from below and from a distance.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100cm diameter; requires significant ceiling height for drop

name: Meridian Brass
placementNotes: Versatile enough for a dining room or a well-appointed study. The antique brass urn body paired with flared ribbed glass shades gives a classic, understated grandeur; suits warm-toned wood furniture.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–65cm diameter; well-proportioned for rooms 12–16ft wide

name: Midnight Cylinder
placementNotes: Best centred over a large dining table or in a double-height living room. The all-black palette pairs well with monochrome interiors or deep-toned walls; avoid light, airy Scandi rooms.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100 cm diameter, suits rooms 14ft+ wide

name: Nautilus Lantern
placementNotes: Ideal for foyers and entrance halls. The bell-jar glass dome and decorative scroll arms make it a strong centrepiece at moderate heights; works well flanked by wall sconces.
scaleReference: Approximately 40–45 cm diameter body, 55–60 cm total drop

name: Cascade
placementNotes: A showstopper for grand double-height spaces or large formal dining rooms. The black iron frame modernises the classic crystal cascade silhouette — pair with dark walls or moody interiors for maximum drama.
scaleReference: Approximately 100–120 cm diameter, requires 12ft+ ceiling clearance

name: Noir Lantern
placementNotes: Best hung in entrances, passages or stairwell nooks. The brass tassel finials at the base add a refined Indian-heritage touch; complements warm wood tones and marble floors.
scaleReference: Approximately 30 cm square, 50–60 cm body height

name: Peacock Dome
placementNotes: Works beautifully as a single statement pendant in a bedroom corner, reading nook, or powder room. The brass peacock figurine is the hero — hang at eye-catching height to ensure the motif reads clearly.
scaleReference: Shade approximately 15–18 cm diameter; pendant with chain approximately 80–100 cm total drop

name: Pleated Pendant
placementNotes: The geometric matte-black frame with warm pleated shades suits transitional and contemporary interiors. Excellent over a large dining table or as a centrepiece in an open-plan living area.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90 cm diameter, two tiers spanning roughly 50 cm height

name: Prism Filigree
placementNotes: Ideally placed in a formal living or dining room with traditional or transitional decor. The antique brass arms and hanging crystal prisms create a warm, sparkling effect — pairs beautifully with warm wood furniture and marble surfaces.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70 cm diameter, suits medium to large rooms

name: Prism Tier
placementNotes: A grand statement piece for double-height foyers or stairwells. The etched crystal bell shades and cascading crystal drops demand generous ceiling height and an unobstructed sightline from below.
scaleReference: Approximately 90–100 cm diameter, requires 12–14ft+ ceiling height

name: Radiant Geometry
placementNotes: The angular spoke-and-hub frame makes a strong architectural statement. Works best in rooms with clean lines and minimal clutter; the warm pleated shades soften the geometric structure for transitional spaces.
scaleReference: Approximately 80–90 cm diameter span across outer arms

name: Royal Crown Pendant
placementNotes: The crown silhouette in distressed white works well in bedrooms, children's rooms, or a powder room with a farmhouse or French country aesthetic. Hang at a lower height to make the crown form legible.
scaleReference: Approximately 35–40 cm diameter, 45–50 cm body height

name: Suspended Foliage
placementNotes: A bespoke ceiling installation featuring hundreds of individually suspended amber and gold glass leaf elements. Best suited for double-height lobbies, grand foyers, or statement staircases. Requires custom structural mounting and professional installation.
scaleReference: Large-scale installation — can span 150–300 cm width depending on configuration

name: Swag Constellation
placementNotes: The polished brass ring with crystal bead swags and contrasting black cylinder shades makes a bold glamorous statement. Best over a round dining table or in a bar area; pairs well with gold-accented interiors.
scaleReference: Approximately 60–70 cm diameter ring, standard drop height

name: Twisted Linen
placementNotes: The heavily ornate twisted iron arms and white bell shades bridge traditional and transitional aesthetics. Well suited to study rooms, dining areas, or heritage-style living spaces; pairs naturally with dark wood furniture and antique accents.
scaleReference: Approximately 70–80 cm diameter, suits medium to large rooms
